title: What Civilization I, II, III Is
body:
Civilization I, II, III is a shorthand for thinking about how human societies change when they scale. Each step changes what counts as power, what the bottlenecks are, and how fast knowledge can travel.

Use the lens as a map, not a law. It is most useful when it helps you compare energy, coordination, and information across eras.
keyPoints: The lens is about scale, not ranking humans
Energy systems and information systems matter at every stage
Stage changes are messy and overlapping
